en.extract:
The black-cowled saltator (Saltator nigriceps) is a seed-eating passerine bird in the tanager family Thraupidae. It is found in the southern border region of Ecuador and the northern border region of Peru. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests and subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.

The '''black-cowled saltator''' ('''''Saltator nigriceps''''') is a seed-eating [[passerine]] bird in the tanager family [[Thraupidae]]. It is found in the southern border region of [[Ecuador]] and the northern border region of [[Peru]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical dry [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.
